_meta.notes: Five-beat arc for the-still-one in Quietmile. The Still One sits at the silence-gate. A visitor reaches the threshold with a name they cannot say (vow of silence). They want the Still One to remember it for them — to take the name into themselves and keep it. The arc tests whether the Still One gives the memory or withholds it; either choice has weight, neither is wrong. Win: presence>=10 AND remembrance>=10 at turn>=8 (you sat through it; the choice held). Lose: remembrance<=0 (the memory was given carelessly, or taken by force).

beats.narrative: A visitor reaches the threshold of the place where you sit. They do not knock; you have not arranged for knocking. The vow of silence makes their approach a slow choreography of small gestures — a hand against the lintel, a bow that is not quite a bow, an in-breath held a beat longer than necessary. They have come a long way for whatever they are about to ask.

suggestedVerbs.label: sit
suggestedVerbs.description: hold your place; let the visitor reach you on their own time

suggestedVerbs.label: regard them
suggestedVerbs.description: look upon them without standing; the looking is its own acknowledgement

suggestedVerbs.label: be unseen for now
suggestedVerbs.description: your presence draws inward; the visitor does not yet know you are here

beats.narrative: The visitor lifts a hand — palm up, fingers loose — and lets a single pressed flower fall into the dust between you. The flower has a name on its stem in pencil so faint you must regard it to read it. They are asking you to remember the name. They cannot say it themselves; the vow holds; but the asking is clear, and the asking is heavy.

suggestedVerbs.label: regard the flower
suggestedVerbs.description: let the name on its stem write itself into your sight

suggestedVerbs.label: permit the asking
suggestedVerbs.description: allow the petition to take its full shape; do not yet answer

suggestedVerbs.label: sit with the asking
suggestedVerbs.description: the silence between you gathers weight; let it gather

beats.narrative: The asking has been made; the asking will not be unmade. The choice is yours. You can take the name into yourself and hold it as long as you sit — that is what the visitor came for, and that is the cost they have already accepted to pay you. Or you can withhold; the name stays on the stem of the flower in the dust, and the visitor must carry it back the way they brought it. Neither answer is wrong. Both have weight.

suggestedVerbs.label: remember the name
suggestedVerbs.description: take it into your remembrance; the visitor's burden becomes yours, by your consent

suggestedVerbs.label: withhold remembrance
suggestedVerbs.description: the name stays on the flower; the visitor carries it home; you have not betrayed them by refusing

suggestedVerbs.label: permit them to ask again
suggestedVerbs.description: the choice is unmade for now; the asking can settle; the answer can wait one more turn of sitting

beats.narrative: The field's silence is older than yours and joins yours without comment. White poppies in slow steady bloom; a cutter passes between rows with a silk pouch, takes three petals, moves on. Whatever you have decided about the visitor's name, the field would not change your decision. It only confirms that you sat with it.

suggestedVerbs.label: abide with the field
suggestedVerbs.description: the silence of the rows joins yours; the sitting deepens

suggestedVerbs.label: regard the cutter
suggestedVerbs.description: they will not regard you back; the gesture is enough

suggestedVerbs.label: let your choice settle
suggestedVerbs.description: whatever you decided in the previous beat firms; you do not unmake it

fires.detail: the field's vow joins the still one's; both deepen

beats.narrative: The visitor stands. They bow — fully this time, with the slowness the vow allows. The pressed flower in the dust has either become part of you or has not, and the visitor knows which without needing to be told. They walk back toward the gate the way they came. Your sitting holds. The threshold is yours again, and so is the silence, and so are the day's small unhurried hours.

suggestedVerbs.label: complete the sitting
suggestedVerbs.description: the visitor leaves; the sitting continues; the day continues; you do

suggestedVerbs.label: regard their leaving
suggestedVerbs.description: look upon their walk back to the gate without standing yourself

suggestedVerbs.label: keep what was kept
suggestedVerbs.description: whatever you decided about the name, the keeping of yourself is intact; the silence holds
